---
title: "Client Report - Exploring Names"
subtitle: "Unit 1 Task 1"
author: "Ethan Neunhoffer"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute: 
  warning: false
    
---

```{python}
import polars as pl
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```

```{python}
url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/byuidatascience/data4names/master/data-raw/names_year/names_year.csv"
df = pl.read_csv(url, infer_schema_length=10000, ignore_errors=True)
```

## QUESTION 1

__What was the earliest year that the name 'Felisha' was used?__

The earliest year that the name Felisha was used is 1964.

```{python}
# Q1
felisha = df.filter(pl.col("name") == "Felisha").sort("year").head(1)
print(felisha)
```


## QUESTION 2

__What year had the most babies named 'David'? How many babies were named 'David' that year?__

The year with the most babies named David was 1955, with 64,754 babies named David that year.

```{python}
# Q2
david = df.filter(pl.col("name") == "David").sort("Total", descending=True).head(1)
print(david)
```


## QUESTION 3

__What year did your name hit its peak? How many babies were named your name in that year?__

The name Ethan peaked in 2004, with 21,640 babies named Ethan that year.

```{python}
# Q3
ethan = df.filter(pl.col("name") == "Ethan").sort("Total", descending=True).head(1)
print(ethan)
```


## QUESTION 4

__In the most recent year, what was the most common female name in Utah?__

In the most recent year of data (2015), the most common female name in Utah was Emma.

```{python}
# Q4
url_prob = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/byuidatascience/data4names/master/data-raw/names_prob/names_prob.csv"
df_prob = pl.read_csv(url_prob, ignore_errors=True)

recent_year = df.select(pl.col("year").max()).item()

utah_female = (
    df.filter(pl.col("year") == recent_year)
    .join(df_prob, on="name", how="left")
    .filter(pl.col("prob_female") > 0.5)
    .sort("UT", descending=True)
    .head(1)
)
print(utah_female)
```


---
